user.title: Input entities
user.description: Select the sensors providing battery SOC and PV forecasts.

data_description.soc_entity: Current battery state of charge in percent (0–100). Basis of all calculations — should update regularly.
data_description.pv_forecast_today_entity: Expected PV energy for today in kWh, e.g. from Solcast or Forecast.Solar.
data_description.pv_forecast_tomorrow_entity: Expected PV energy for tomorrow in kWh.
data_description.pv_forecast_day_after_entity: Expected PV energy for the day after tomorrow in kWh — extends the planning horizon to up to 3 days.

data_description.battery_capacity_wh: Total battery capacity in watt-hours (e.g. 5000 for 5 kWh).
data_description.battery_min_soc_percent: Hard lower limit: planning never discharges the battery below this state of charge (deep-discharge protection).
data_description.battery_max_soc_percent: Upper charging limit: above this value the battery absorbs no more surplus (protects the cells).
data_description.battery_charge_efficiency: Share of charging energy that actually ends up in the battery (0.97 = 3 % charging losses).
data_description.battery_discharge_efficiency: Share of stored energy that is usable when discharging (0.97 = 3 % discharging losses).

data_description.pv_max_power_w: Maximum PV system power — caps the hourly values derived from the daily forecast.
data_description.pv_morning_start_hour: Hour from which the system produces noticeably (7 = from 07:00).
data_description.pv_morning_end_hour: End of the high-yield morning window — the morning share configured below falls into this window.
data_description.pv_afternoon_end_hour: Hour at which production ends (18 = until 18:00).
data_description.pv_morning_ratio: Share of the daily energy produced in the morning window (0.8 = 80 % mornings, 20 % afternoons). Adjust to your panel orientation.

consumption_profile.name: Consumption profile (static fallback)
consumption_profile.description: Used until learned consumption data is available.

data_description.ac_base_load_w: Permanent round-the-clock AC consumption of the household (fridge, router, standby …).
data_description.ac_variable_load_w: Additional AC consumption occurring only within the time window configured below (daytime activity).
data_description.ac_variable_start_hour: Start of the window in which the additional AC load occurs.
data_description.ac_variable_end_hour: End of the window in which the additional AC load occurs.
data_description.dc_base_load_w: Permanent consumption on the DC rail (24 V), supplied directly from the battery.
data_description.dc_variable_load_w: Additional DC consumption occurring only within the time window configured below.
data_description.dc_variable_start_hour: Start of the window in which the additional DC load occurs.
data_description.dc_variable_end_hour: End of the window in which the additional DC load occurs.

consumption_learning.name: Measurement sources for learning (optional)
consumption_learning.description: Optional sensors that enable the nightly profile learning.

data.ac_load_entity: AC load sensor for learned profile (optional)
data.dc_load_entity: DC load sensor for learned profile (optional)

data_description.ac_load_entity: Sensor measuring the AC consumer load directly (W sensor or kWh counter). Enables nightly learning of the hourly AC profile from long-term statistics; takes precedence over the counter balance. Important: do not pick the grid import or any point that includes the system's own battery charging.
data_description.ac_balance_in_entities: Alternative without a direct load sensor: counters for energy flowing into the AC consumption node (e.g. grid import, inverter output, PV passthrough). House load = inflows − outflows.
data_description.ac_balance_out_entities: Counters for energy leaving the node unconsumed (e.g. grid export, battery charging from the house bus).
data_description.dc_load_entity: Sensor for the DC consumer load (e.g. shunt). Enables learning of the DC profile; without it the DC profile stays static.
data_description.dc_balance_in_entities: Alternative without a direct DC load sensor: inflow counters of the DC consumption node.
data_description.dc_balance_out_entities: Outflow counters of the DC consumption node (energy that is not consumed).
data_description.workday_entity: Binary sensor of the Workday integration (on = workday). Holidays are then learned and planned like weekends; upcoming horizon days are checked via workday.check_date.

data_description.charger_max_power_w: Maximum AC input power of the battery charger — limits how fast surplus can be stored.
data_description.charger_efficiency: AC→DC conversion efficiency of the charger (0.92 = 8 % loss).
data_description.inverter_max_power_w: Maximum AC output power of the discharge inverter.
data_description.inverter_efficiency: DC→AC conversion efficiency of the inverter (0.95 = 5 % loss).
data_description.inverter_min_soc_percent: Below this state of charge the inverter shuts off hard — the house then runs from the grid.

control.title: Planner & emergency support
control.description: Planner tuning and optional grid support paths that the integration switches directly.

planner_tuning.name: Control parameters
planner_tuning.description: SOC buffer, hysteresis and switching intervals of the planner.

data_description.soc_buffer_percent: Safety reserve above the battery minimum: planning keeps this buffer free for forecast errors (recommendation: 5 %).
data_description.hysteresis_percent: Dead band around the SOC threshold: the inverter recommendation only changes once the SOC crosses the threshold by this amount — prevents rapid toggling.
data_description.threshold_inertia_percent: A new threshold is only adopted if it differs from the previous one by at least this amount — prevents jumping between equivalent plans.
data_description.min_switch_interval_s: Minimum time between two real switching actions (inverter recommendation and support paths).

support_paths.name: Support paths (switches)
support_paths.description: Emergency DC-rail support via grid-fed PSUs.

data_description.support_dc48_switch_entity: Optional: switch of the PSU that feeds the 48 V battery bus with fixed power in an emergency. Switched directly by the integration.
data_description.support_dc48_power_w: Fixed feed-in power of the 48 V support PSU used by the simulation.
data_description.support_dc24_switch_entity: Optional: switch of the 24 V PSU that supplies the DC loads from the grid in an emergency (instead of via the DC/DC converter from the battery). Switched directly.
data_description.dcdc_switch_entity: Optional: switch of the 48 V → 24 V DC/DC converter. Enables make-before-break changeover: new source on first, then old source off — the 24 V rail is never unpowered.
data_description.support_switch_delay_s: Wait time between switching the new 24 V source on and the old one off during changeover.
data_description.support_dc24_power_entity: Measures the 24 V grid PSU's power draw. Makes the DC→AC load shift during PSU operation exactly correctable for consumption learning — without it, hours with a PSU-fed 24 V rail cannot be learned (relevant when the PSU runs permanently in winter).
data_description.support_dc24_activate_soc: Battery SOC at or below which the 24 V grid PSU takes over the DC rail from the DC/DC converter (a last-resort protection level, independent of the planning buffer). Default 10 %.
data_description.support_dc24_recovery_soc: Battery SOC at or above which the 24 V grid support switches back off. Set it a few percent above the activate level: a wider gap latches the support on longer and stops it chattering when the SOC parks on the threshold overnight. Default 11 %.
data_description.support_dc48_activate_soc: Battery SOC at or below which the last-resort 48 V support PSU also engages (deepest protection). Keep it at or below the 24 V activate level. Default 5.5 %.
data_description.support_dc48_recovery_soc: Battery SOC at or above which the 48 V support switches back off. Must be above the 48 V activate level and at or below the 24 V recover level. Default 10 %.

dc_devices.name: DC device parameters (two-bus)
dc_devices.description: Efficiencies, currents, voltages and the voltage gate of the DC topology.

data_description.battery_voltage_entity: 48 V battery voltage sensor — for the gate calibration and (later) the 48 V PSU voltage controller.
data_description.native48_base_w: A fixed, roughly constant load wired directly to the 48 V bus (e.g. ~35 W). Carved off BEFORE the rail split — more accurate than a percentage, which would scale with the total DC load. 0 = none.
data_description.dc24_share_percent: Fraction of the REMAINING DC load (after the fixed 48 V base) on the 24 V rail (rest = native 48 V bus). 100 % = all remaining DC load on the rail (as before).
data_description.dcdc_output_voltage_v: DC/DC converter output voltage. When it runs in parallel with the 24 V PSU, the higher-voltage source feeds the rail.
data_description.dcdc_max_current_a: DC/DC converter max output current. 0 = uncapped. Power cap = voltage × current.
data_description.psu24_output_voltage_v: Output voltage of the grid-fed 24 V support PSU.
data_description.psu24_max_current_a: 24 V PSU max output current. 0 = uncapped.
data_description.psu48_output_voltage_v: Fixed output voltage of the 48 V support PSU (e.g. 49.56 V). Defines the voltage gate.
data_description.psu48_max_current_a: 48 V PSU max output current. 0 = uncapped.
data_description.battery_cells_series: Series cell count (e.g. 15 for Pylontech US5000). Informational: derives volts per cell as a calibration aid.
data_description.gate_soc_percent: The 48 V PSU only delivers below this SOC (proxy for the voltage threshold). 100 % = always open (off). Read the value from the gate calibration on the SOC forecast sensor.
data_description.psu48_on_voltage_v: In manual 48 V mode: below this battery voltage the controller switches the PSU ON. Requires the battery voltage sensor.
data_description.psu48_off_voltage_v: In manual 48 V mode: at or above this battery voltage the controller switches the PSU OFF. Must be above the turn-on voltage (hysteresis).
data_description.psu48_controller_log_only: On = controller only logs its decisions without switching (dry run). Off = controller actively switches the 48 V PSU.

error.min_soc_above_max: Minimum SOC must be below maximum SOC.
error.pv_windows_out_of_order: The PV windows must be strictly ordered: morning start < morning end < afternoon end.
error.support_entities_not_distinct: The 24 V PSU, 48 V PSU and DC/DC converter must use different switch entities.
error.balance_out_without_in: Outflow counters without inflow counters: the balance needs at least one inflow entity.
error.buffer_min_above_max: The dynamic buffer minimum must be below the maximum.
error.controller_off_below_on: The 48 V controller turn-off voltage must be above the turn-on voltage.
error.support_dc24_recovery_not_above_activate: The 24 V support recover-SOC must be above its activate-SOC (a hysteresis dead band is required).
error.support_dc48_recovery_not_above_activate: The 48 V support recover-SOC must be above its activate-SOC (a hysteresis dead band is required).
error.support_dc48_activate_above_dc24: The 48 V support activate-SOC must be at or below the 24 V activate-SOC (the last-resort stage engages no later than the 24 V support).
error.support_dc48_recovery_above_dc24: The 48 V support recover-SOC must be at or below the 24 V recover-SOC.

error.keep_on_requires_enable: The 'keep on' policy requires a charge-enable entity — otherwise charging could never be stopped.
error.control_entities_not_distinct: Charging input switch and charge-enable entity must be different.

user.title: Surplus load
user.description: A switchable load that should consume PV surplus (powerstation, dehumidifier, ...). Priority follows the order of creation.

data_description.name: Display name of the load — appears in the sub-entry and in the recommendation entity's name.
data_description.power_w: Typical power draw used for planning. For storage loads use the (possibly throttled) charging power, e.g. 300 W.
data_description.battery_tolerance_percent: How much of the load's hourly energy may come from the home battery (bridges e.g. cloud gaps). 0 = genuine surplus only. Additional grid import is never caused regardless.
data_description.min_runtime_min: Minimum on/off dwell time of the real switching — protects the device and relays.
data_description.energy_limited: Enable if the load is a storage that becomes full (e.g. a powerstation). Off = continuous consumer such as a dehumidifier.
data_description.power_entity: Sensor with the actually measured power draw. Replaces (smoothed) the nominal power in planning once readings are available.
data_description.availability_entity: The load is only scheduled while this entity is on/available — e.g. an input_boolean as a manual enable switch. off/unavailable/unknown = the load is ignored.
data_description.in_house_measurement: Keep enabled if this load sits behind the configured consumption measuring point — it is then subtracted while learning the baseline. Disable if the load is supplied outside of it (e.g. via a feed-in setpoint): otherwise it would be subtracted twice.
data_description.power_warning_percent: Warning threshold for the deviation between real draw and the configured power while the load runs at the integration's request. If the deviation persists for more than 30 minutes (e.g. full water tank: near 0 W), the load's warning sensor turns on. 0 = disabled. Short defrost pauses stay below the 30 minutes and do not trigger.

data_description.name: Display name of the load — appears in the sub-entry and in the recommendation entity's name.
data_description.power_w: Typical power draw used for planning. For storage loads use the (possibly throttled) charging power, e.g. 300 W.
data_description.battery_tolerance_percent: How much of the load's hourly energy may come from the home battery (bridges e.g. cloud gaps). 0 = genuine surplus only. Additional grid import is never caused regardless.
data_description.min_runtime_min: Minimum on/off dwell time of the real switching — protects the device and relays.
data_description.energy_limited: Enable if the load is a storage that becomes full (e.g. a powerstation). Off = continuous consumer such as a dehumidifier.
data_description.power_entity: Sensor with the actually measured power draw. Replaces (smoothed) the nominal power in planning once readings are available.
data_description.availability_entity: The load is only scheduled while this entity is on/available — e.g. an input_boolean as a manual enable switch. off/unavailable/unknown = the load is ignored.
data_description.in_house_measurement: Keep enabled if this load sits behind the configured consumption measuring point — it is then subtracted while learning the baseline. Disable if the load is supplied outside of it (e.g. via a feed-in setpoint): otherwise it would be subtracted twice.
data_description.power_warning_percent: Warning threshold for the deviation between real draw and the configured power while the load runs at the integration's request. If the deviation persists for more than 30 minutes (e.g. full water tank: near 0 W), the load's warning sensor turns on. 0 = disabled. Short defrost pauses stay below the 30 minutes and do not trigger.

storage.title: Storage settings
storage.description: These fields only apply to energy-limited loads (a storage that fills up, e.g. a power station): capacity, target SOC, SOC sensor and the charging path (input switch, charge enable, input-off policy).

data.control_switch_entity: Charging input switch (optional — the integration switches it directly)
data.input_off_policy: Input plug at end of charge

data_description.capacity_wh: Storage size of the load (only relevant if energy limited), e.g. 2000 for a 2 kWh powerstation.
data_description.target_soc_percent: The load is charged up to this state of charge; above it, it counts as full and is no longer scheduled.
data_description.soc_entity: State-of-charge sensor of the load. If the device sleeps (sensor unavailable), planning continues with the last known value.
data_description.control_switch_entity: Switchable plug of the charging input. If set, the integration switches charging itself — a separate charging automation becomes redundant and should be disabled.
data_description.charge_enable_entity: "Battery may charge" gate: switched together with the input. Allows powering the powerstation's output from the grid (passthrough) without charging the battery.
data_description.input_off_policy: What happens to the input plug at the end of charging. "Automatic" = only switch off if the integration switched it on itself — recommended when the input is also used for passthrough.

user.title: Appliance
user.description: Washer/dishwasher: a detected run adds its remaining consumption to the forecast; optionally a start-window advisor entity is created.

data_description.name: Display name of the appliance.
data_description.detection_entity: Entity that indicates a running program: either a power sensor (W) or a state sensor (e.g. "running").
data_description.power_threshold_w: Only relevant for power sensors: above this power the appliance counts as running.
data_description.run_energy_wh: Energy consumption of a typical program — added to the forecast as remaining consumption when a run is detected.
data_description.run_duration_h: Duration of a typical program in hours (e.g. 3.5).
data_description.opportunistic_start: Creates an additional entity that indicates when a complete run could start right now without any grid import — useful as a start signal.

data_description.name: Display name of the appliance.
data_description.detection_entity: Entity that indicates a running program: either a power sensor (W) or a state sensor (e.g. "running").
data_description.power_threshold_w: Only relevant for power sensors: above this power the appliance counts as running.
data_description.run_energy_wh: Energy consumption of a typical program — added to the forecast as remaining consumption when a run is detected.
data_description.run_duration_h: Duration of a typical program in hours (e.g. 3.5).
data_description.opportunistic_start: Creates an additional entity that indicates when a complete run could start right now without any grid import — useful as a start signal.

error.support_entities_not_distinct: The 24 V PSU, 48 V PSU and DC/DC converter must use different switch entities.
error.balance_out_without_in: Outflow counters without inflow counters: the balance needs at least one inflow entity.
error.buffer_min_above_max: The dynamic buffer minimum must be below the maximum.
error.controller_off_below_on: The 48 V controller turn-off voltage must be above the turn-on voltage.
error.support_dc24_recovery_not_above_activate: The 24 V support recover-SOC must be above its activate-SOC (a hysteresis dead band is required).
error.support_dc48_recovery_not_above_activate: The 48 V support recover-SOC must be above its activate-SOC (a hysteresis dead band is required).
error.support_dc48_activate_above_dc24: The 48 V support activate-SOC must be at or below the 24 V activate-SOC (the last-resort stage engages no later than the 24 V support).
error.support_dc48_recovery_above_dc24: The 48 V support recover-SOC must be at or below the 24 V recover-SOC.

planner_tuning.name: Control parameters
planner_tuning.description: SOC buffer, hysteresis and switching intervals of the planner.

data_description.soc_buffer_percent: Safety reserve above the battery minimum: planning keeps this buffer free for forecast errors (recommendation: 5 %).
data_description.hysteresis_percent: Dead band around the SOC threshold: the inverter recommendation only changes once the SOC crosses the threshold by this amount — prevents rapid toggling.
data_description.threshold_inertia_percent: A new threshold is only adopted if it differs from the previous one by at least this amount — prevents jumping between equivalent plans.
data_description.min_switch_interval_s: Minimum time between two real switching actions (inverter recommendation and support paths).

consumption_profile.name: Consumption profile (static fallback)
consumption_profile.description: Used until learned consumption data is available.

data_description.ac_base_load_w: Permanent round-the-clock AC consumption of the household (fridge, router, standby …).
data_description.ac_variable_load_w: Additional AC consumption occurring only within the time window configured below (daytime activity).
data_description.ac_variable_start_hour: Start of the window in which the additional AC load occurs.
data_description.ac_variable_end_hour: End of the window in which the additional AC load occurs.
data_description.dc_base_load_w: Permanent consumption on the DC rail (24 V), supplied directly from the battery.
data_description.dc_variable_load_w: Additional DC consumption occurring only within the time window configured below.
data_description.dc_variable_start_hour: Start of the window in which the additional DC load occurs.
data_description.dc_variable_end_hour: End of the window in which the additional DC load occurs.

consumption_learning.name: Consumption learning & measurement sources
consumption_learning.description: Measurement sources and parameters for the nightly profile learning.

data.ac_load_entity: AC load sensor for learned profile (optional)
data.dc_load_entity: DC load sensor for learned profile (optional)

data_description.ac_load_entity: Sensor measuring the AC consumer load directly (W sensor or kWh counter). Enables nightly learning of the hourly AC profile from long-term statistics; takes precedence over the counter balance. Important: do not pick the grid import or any point that includes the system's own battery charging.
data_description.ac_balance_in_entities: Alternative without a direct load sensor: counters for energy flowing into the AC consumption node (e.g. grid import, inverter output, PV passthrough). House load = inflows − outflows.
data_description.ac_balance_out_entities: Counters for energy leaving the node unconsumed (e.g. grid export, battery charging from the house bus).
data_description.dc_load_entity: Sensor for the DC consumer load (e.g. shunt). Enables learning of the DC profile; without it the DC profile stays static.
data_description.dc_balance_in_entities: Alternative without a direct DC load sensor: inflow counters of the DC consumption node.
data_description.dc_balance_out_entities: Outflow counters of the DC consumption node (energy that is not consumed).
data_description.workday_entity: Binary sensor of the Workday integration (on = workday). Holidays are then learned and planned like weekends; upcoming horizon days are checked via workday.check_date.
data_description.learning_window_days: Rolling window the hourly profiles are learned from. Larger = more stable but slower to follow behaviour changes.
data_description.learning_max_age_days: If the last successful learning run is older, forecasting falls back entirely to the static profile (fail-safe).
data_description.profile_half_life_days: Weighting of learning days: a day of this age still counts half. Replaces an explicit season model — smaller values follow behaviour changes faster, larger ones smooth more.
data_description.buffer_min_percent: Lower clamp of the dynamic safety buffer derived from the forecast uncertainty (P80−P50).
data_description.buffer_max_percent: Upper clamp of the dynamic safety buffer — limits how much reserve the uncertainty may request.

support_paths.name: Support paths (switches)
support_paths.description: Emergency DC-rail support via grid-fed PSUs.

data_description.support_dc48_switch_entity: Optional: switch of the PSU that feeds the 48 V battery bus with fixed power in an emergency. Switched directly by the integration. Clear the field to disable.
data_description.support_dc48_power_w: Fixed feed-in power of the 48 V support PSU used by the simulation.
data_description.support_dc24_switch_entity: Optional: switch of the 24 V PSU that supplies the DC loads from the grid in an emergency (instead of via the DC/DC converter from the battery). Clear the field to disable.
data_description.dcdc_switch_entity: Optional: switch of the 48 V → 24 V DC/DC converter. Enables make-before-break changeover: new source on first, then old source off — the 24 V rail is never unpowered.
data_description.support_switch_delay_s: Wait time between switching the new 24 V source on and the old one off during changeover.
data_description.support_dc24_power_entity: Measures the 24 V grid PSU's power draw. Makes the DC→AC load shift during PSU operation exactly correctable for consumption learning — without it, hours with a PSU-fed 24 V rail cannot be learned (relevant when the PSU runs permanently in winter).
data_description.support_dc24_activate_soc: Battery SOC at or below which the 24 V grid PSU takes over the DC rail from the DC/DC converter (a last-resort protection level, independent of the planning buffer). Default 10 %.
data_description.support_dc24_recovery_soc: Battery SOC at or above which the 24 V grid support switches back off. Set it a few percent above the activate level: a wider gap latches the support on longer and stops it chattering when the SOC parks on the threshold overnight. Default 11 %.
data_description.support_dc48_activate_soc: Battery SOC at or below which the last-resort 48 V support PSU also engages (deepest protection). Keep it at or below the 24 V activate level. Default 5.5 %.
data_description.support_dc48_recovery_soc: Battery SOC at or above which the 48 V support switches back off. Must be above the 48 V activate level and at or below the 24 V recover level. Default 10 %.

dc_devices.name: DC device parameters (two-bus)
dc_devices.description: Efficiencies, currents, voltages and the voltage gate of the DC topology.

data_description.battery_voltage_entity: 48 V battery voltage sensor — for the gate calibration and (later) the 48 V PSU voltage controller.
data_description.native48_base_w: A fixed, roughly constant load wired directly to the 48 V bus (e.g. ~35 W). Carved off BEFORE the rail split — more accurate than a percentage, which would scale with the total DC load. 0 = none.
data_description.dc24_share_percent: Fraction of the REMAINING DC load (after the fixed 48 V base) on the 24 V rail (rest = native 48 V bus). 100 % = all remaining DC load on the rail (as before).
data_description.dcdc_output_voltage_v: DC/DC converter output voltage. When it runs in parallel with the 24 V PSU, the higher-voltage source feeds the rail.
data_description.dcdc_max_current_a: DC/DC converter max output current. 0 = uncapped. Power cap = voltage × current.
data_description.psu24_output_voltage_v: Output voltage of the grid-fed 24 V support PSU.
data_description.psu24_max_current_a: 24 V PSU max output current. 0 = uncapped.
data_description.psu48_output_voltage_v: Fixed output voltage of the 48 V support PSU (e.g. 49.56 V). Defines the voltage gate.
data_description.psu48_max_current_a: 48 V PSU max output current. 0 = uncapped.
data_description.battery_cells_series: Series cell count (e.g. 15 for Pylontech US5000). Informational: derives volts per cell as a calibration aid.
data_description.gate_soc_percent: The 48 V PSU only delivers below this SOC (proxy for the voltage threshold). 100 % = always open (off). Read the value from the gate calibration on the SOC forecast sensor.
data_description.psu48_on_voltage_v: In manual 48 V mode: below this battery voltage the controller switches the PSU ON. Requires the battery voltage sensor.
data_description.psu48_off_voltage_v: In manual 48 V mode: at or above this battery voltage the controller switches the PSU OFF. Must be above the turn-on voltage (hysteresis).
data_description.psu48_controller_log_only: On = controller only logs its decisions without switching (dry run). Off = controller actively switches the 48 V PSU.

options.auto: Automatic (off only if the integration switched it on)

learning_no_statistics.title: Consumption learning: long-term statistics missing
learning_no_statistics.description: The following measurement entities have no long-term statistics (missing state_class or excluded from the recorder): {entity_ids}. The affected path keeps using the static load profile.

learning_bias.title: Consumption learning: systematic bias
learning_bias.description: The learned P50 consumption forecast has deviated one-sidedly from the measured load for more than two weeks. Check the measuring point, the loads' in-house flags and the cleaning sensors (diagnostics: consumption_profile on the SOC forecast sensor, service export_learned_profiles).
